title: myriopteris tomentosa
intro: Myriopteris tomentosa, also known as the woolly polypody fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Myriopteris tomentosa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and promote digestive health. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional folk medicine.
